commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

issues.description: Task title on line 105-117 is clickable text (as={Clickable}) without keyboard navigation support — add tabIndex and keyboard event handler (Enter/Space) to trigger edit mode
issues.fix: Task title on line 105-117 is clickable text (as={Clickable}) without keyboard navigation support — add tabIndex and keyboard event handler (Enter/Space) to trigger edit mode

issues.description: Summary bar (line 246) uses descriptive text but 'Task Summary' should be a semantic heading (h2 or h3) instead of a label for screen reader users to properly navigate landmarks
issues.fix: Summary bar (line 246) uses descriptive text but 'Task Summary' should be a semantic heading (h2 or h3) instead of a label for screen reader users to properly navigate landmarks

issues.description: Line 114: style={{ flex: 1, cursor: 'pointer' }} uses inline style object bypassing design system. Consider using a utility class or component variant.
issues.fix: Line 114: style={{ flex: 1, cursor: 'pointer' }} uses inline style object bypassing design system. Consider using a utility class or component variant.

issues.description: Line 273: style={{ minWidth: '320px', maxWidth: '400px', flex: '1 1 320px', minHeight: '600px', display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column' }} uses hardcoded pixel values (320px, 400px, 600px) instead of design system spacing/sizing tokens. Should use var(--ggui-spacing-*) or design system size tokens.

issues.description: Line 289-293: style={{ flex: 1, overflowY: 'auto', paddingRight: 'var(--ggui-spacing-1)' }} mixes inline styles; while paddingRight correctly uses a token, flex and overflowY should be handled through component props or design system utilities.
issues.fix: Line 289-293: style={{ flex: 1, overflowY: 'auto', paddingRight: 'var(--ggui-spacing-1)' }} mixes inline styles; while paddingRight correctly uses a token, flex and overflowY should be handled through component props or design system utilities.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid use of design tokens and proper layout structure with Kanban columns, but lacks visual separation between columns, has incomplete stream handling logic (cut off), and missing hover/focus polish on interactive elements. The edit mode and move dropdown work well, but the overall polish for drag-and-drop indication and loading states could be stronger.
